reasoning: The knowledge cutoff is November 9, 2025, leaving roughly 52 days in the year. Bitcoin reached an all-time high of $125,689 on October 5, 2025, meaning it only needs an ~11.4% increase from that ATH to trigger the $140,000 condition. However, it's common for BTC to experience pullbacks after a new ATH, so the price as of early November may be lower (e.g., $110k–$120k), requiring a 16–27% surge in under two months. While historical post-halving bull markets frequently peak in Q4 (e.g., November or December), and an expansion from $125k to $140k is well within typical cryptocurrency volatility, the timeframe is short and there is a significant risk that the peak for the cycle was either already set in October or that consolidation will delay the next leg up until 2026. Taking the distance to the target, standard implied volatility estimates (~60% annualized), and historical market structures into account, the probability of reaching a $140k high on the 1-minute chart before year's end is modeled as a moderate but not highly favored scenario.
